2604.24473.title: Agentic clinical reasoning over longitudinal myeloma records: a retrospective evaluation against expert consensus
2604.24473.paper_summary: Multiple myeloma is managed through sequential lines of therapy over years to decades, with each decision depending on cumulative disease history distributed across dozens to hundreds of heterogeneous clinical documents. Whether LLM-based systems can synthesise this evidence at a level approaching expert agreement has not been established. A retrospective evaluation was conducted on longitudinal clinical records of 811 myeloma patients treated at a tertiary centre (2001-2026), covering 44,962 documents and 1,334,677 laboratory values, with external validation on MIMIC-IV. An agentic reasoning system was compared against single-pass retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), iterative RAG, and full-context input on 469 patient-question pairs from 48 templates at three complexity levels. Reference labels came from double annotation by four oncologists with senior haematologist adjudication. Iterative RAG and full-context input converged on a shared ceiling (75.4% vs 75.8%, p = 1.00). The agentic system reached 79.6% concordance (95% CI 76.4-82.8), exceeding both baselines (+3.8 and +4.2 pp; p = 0.006 and 0.007). Gains rose with question complexity, reaching +9.4 pp on criteria-based synthesis (p = 0.032), and with record length, reaching +13.5 pp in the top decile (n = 10). The system error rate (12.2%) was comparable to expert disagreement (13.6%), but severity was inverted: 57.8% of system errors were clinically significant versus 18.8% of expert disagreements. Agentic reasoning was the only approach to exceed the shared ceiling, with gains concentrated on the most complex questions and longest records. The greater clinical consequence of residual system errors indicates that prospective evaluation in routine care is required before these findings translate into patient benefit.
2604.24473.paper_summary_zh: 多發性骨髓瘤的管理需要通過數年到數十年的多個治療階段，每個決策都取決於分佈在數十到數百份異質臨床文件中的累積疾病歷史。基於大型語言模型（LLM）的系統是否能在接近專家共識的水平上綜合這些證據尚未確定。對於在一個三級醫療中心（2001-2026）治療的811名骨髓瘤患者的縱向臨床記錄進行了回顧性評估，涵蓋了44,962份文件和1,334,677個實驗室數據，並在MIMIC-IV上進行了外部驗證。將一個代理推理系統與單次檢索增強生成（RAG）、迭代RAG和全上下文輸入進行比較，基於來自48個模板的469個患者問題對，涵蓋三個複雜性級別。參考標籤來自四位腫瘤學家的雙重標註，並由資深血液學家裁定。迭代RAG和全上下文輸入在共享上限上達成了一致（75.4%對75.8%，p = 1.00）。代理系統達到了79.6%的一致性（95% CI 76.4-82.8），超過了兩個基準（+3.8和+4.2個百分點；p = 0.006和0.007）。隨著問題複雜性的增加，增益上升，在基於標準的綜合中達到+9.4個百分點（p = 0.032），而隨著記錄長度的增加，在最高十分位中達到+13.5個百分點（n = 10）。該系統的錯誤率（12.2%）與專家分歧（13.6%）相當，但嚴重性則相反：57.8%的系統錯誤在臨床上具有重要意義，而專家分歧中則為18.8%。代理推理是唯一一種超過共享上限的方法，增益集中在最複雜的問題和最長的記錄上。剩餘系統錯誤的更大臨床後果表明，在這些發現轉化為患者利益之前，需要在常規護理中進行前瞻性評估。
2604.24473.author: Johannes Moll et.al.
2604.24473.authors: Johannes Moll, Jannik Lübberstedt, Christoph Nuernbergk, Jacob Stroh, Luisa Mertens, Anna Purcarea, Christopher Zirn, Zeineb Benchaaben, Fabian Drexel, Hartmut Häntze, Anirudh Narayanan, Friedrich Puttkammer, Andrei Zhukov, Jacqueline Lammert, Sebastian Ziegelmayer, Markus Graf, Marion Högner, Marcus Makowski, Florian Bassermann, Lisa C. Adams, Jiazhen Pan, Daniel Rueckert, Krischan Braitsch, Keno K. Bressem
